mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate lower the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not statistically higher than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and moderate inflation indicates robust aggregate demand without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, creating a favorable environment for equity valuation expansion over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in over 60% of occurrences, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in quarterly regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and rising business investment, which boosts short-term market sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 returns within one trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 58% of triggered periods, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income increases household purchasing power and drives higher corporate revenues, establishing a persistent positive correlation with S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all 3-year rolling windows, or 20-day returns show no statistical relationship with income growth (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading crossing above 3.0 percent forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in over 55% of high-inflation instances, or the negative correlation vanishes (Rank IC > -0.01) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market that supports consumer confidence and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal yields negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate models.

mechanism_narrative: Surges in new housing starts indicate rising consumer wealth and construction sector activity, which amplifies short-term market optimism and produces positive S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in over 57% of high-housing-start periods, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below chance during housing upturns.
